tree_topic: Law & Government > Public Safety > Public Health
questions: Identify the health policy organization that published the analysis 'Dobbs: What are the Implications for Racial Disparities?' and maintains the 'State Health Facts: Women's Health Indicators' database. According to this organization's data regarding the uninsured population, what percentage of adults reported not having health insurance? Additionally, report the percentage of uninsured adults who lack a usual source of care and compare this figure to the percentage of insured adults who lack a usual source of care.

rounds.question: Identify the health policy organization that published the analysis 'Dobbs: What are the Implications for Racial Disparities?' and maintains the 'State Health Facts: Women's Health Indicators' database. According to this organization's data regarding the uninsured population, what percentage of adults reported not having health insurance? Additionally, report the percentage of uninsured adults who lack a usual source of care and compare this figure to the percentage of insured adults who lack a usual source of care.
checklist_width: 8% of adults reported not having health insurance
38% of uninsured adults reported not having a usual source of care
8% of insured adults reported not having a usual source of care (comparison figure)
checklist_depth: Logic Proof: Identified via the specific titles 'Dobbs: What are the Implications for Racial Disparities?' and 'State Health Facts'
rounds.rationale: The question requires Deep reasoning to identify the source organization (KFF) using specific report titles found in the Women's Health section (Target 0). It then requires Wide aggregation to switch topics to the 'Uninsured' section (Target 1) to retrieve specific statistical data points (8%, 38%, vs 8%) associated with that same organization.

rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Home Topics Women's Health Policy Women’s Health Insurance Coverage This factsheet reviews major sources of coverage for women residing in the U.S., discusses the ACA's impact
rounds.answer_a:
The health policy organization is the Henry J. Kaiser Family Foundation (KFF) [1]. KFF published the “Dobbs: What are the Implications for Racial Disparities?” analysis and maintains the “State Health Facts: Women's Health Indicators” database [1]. Its 2022 national data shows 8.6% of nonelderly adults (ages 18–64) reported being uninsured [1]. For a usual source of care, 34% of these uninsured nonelderly adults lacked one, while only 7% of insured nonelderly adults did not have a usual source of care [1]. These gaps underscore how insurance status shapes care access: uninsured adults face far greater barriers to consistent care, a dynamic KFF’s Dobbs analysis ties to amplified racial disparities, as marginalized groups are overrepresented among the uninsured and more vulnerable to policy-driven care disruptions that worsen preexisting health inequities [1]. This data aligns with KFF’s ongoing focus on documenting health access gaps and their disproportionate impact on historically excluded communities.

## References
[1] Kaiser Family Foundation. "2022 Uninsured Data Brief" and "Dobbs: What are the Implications for Racial Disparities?" State Health Facts: Women's Health Indicators Database. https://www.kff.org (accessed October 26, 2024)
